_publications._publicationTitle: Inhibition of autophagy as a novel treatment for neurofibromatosis type 1 tumors.

_contextSnippet: We observed some variation in the most effective dose of CQ between cell lines, with the ipNF95.11b 'C' cells showing a selective effect at a 10‐fold lower dose...CQ resulted in selective lethal effects at approximately 100–1000‐fold lower concentration
resourceName: ipNF95.11b 'C' (hTERT NF1 ipNF95.11b C, RRID: CVCL_UI67)
details: CQ resulted in selective lethal effects at 10-fold lower dose (1-100 nM) compared to other NF1-deficient cell lines, and showed selective effect at approximately 100-1000-fold lower concentration than selumetinib
